outlook.headline: Chiplet adoption is broadening, led by EDA and advanced packaging
outlook.summary: Company reads point to a modestly improving chiplet outlook over the next 1-2 quarters, with the clearest pull coming from design software and advanced packaging tools rather than end-market demand. Cadence and Synopsys see stronger 3D IC/die-to-die pull, while Applied Materials, ACM Research, and Onto benefit from tighter advanced packaging, metrology, and yield-management needs; Marvell’s first-generation chiplet moving toward high-volume manufacturing adds evidence of real commercialization. Mention activity is still only 0.3x and just above trend, so adoption looks early rather than explosive, and the more consumer-facing names remain noncommittal.

drivers.label: 3D IC and die-to-die demand is rising
drivers.detail: Chiplet-based architectures are explicitly cited as a demand driver for EDA and multi-die solutions, supporting near-term tool demand.

drivers.label: Advanced packaging demand remains tight
drivers.detail: 3D chiplet stacking and AI-driven 3D IC packaging are called hot, supporting equipment demand in the packaging flow.

drivers.label: Metrology and yield control needs increase
drivers.detail: Chiplet mainstreaming raises complexity and yield concerns, which supports inspection/metrology spending.

drivers.label: Chiplet moves from concept to HVM
drivers.detail: A first-generation chiplet entering high-volume manufacturing suggests customer acceptance and a path to growth.

drivers.label: End-demand read-through is still limited
drivers.detail: Mentions from some connectivity/handset names are too vague or purely conceptual, implying adoption is not yet broad across all customers.

risks: Chiplet adoption remains uneven if more end-market names stay conceptual rather than confirming design wins or volume ramps.
Any easing in advanced-packaging tightness would reduce the urgency for packaging, metrology, and EDA spend.
A delayed HVM ramp for first-generation chiplet programs would slow the conversion of interest into revenue.
